title: Levant-Iran Escalation Chain: From Damascus to Hormuz
learning_objectives: Practice crisis escalation management across direct state-on-state and proxy warfare.
Evaluate how air defense, coalition cohesion, legitimacy, and nuclear risk interact in a multi-theater conflict.
Understand how maritime chokepoint coercion can internationalize a regional war.
Assess the limits of decapitation strikes and the strategic tradeoffs of regime-change campaigns.

players.label: United State of America
players.victory_condition: Prevent Iranian nuclear breakout, preserve coalition credibility, keep regional trade flowing, and avoid uncontrolled escalation or regime-collapse blowback.
players.notes: Core crisis manager and primary escalatory backstop for Israel.

players.label: NATO and European Union
players.victory_condition: Sustain coalition unity, blunt Iranian retaliation, limit humanitarian/legal fallout, and support de-escalation or ceasefire terms favorable to Blue.
players.notes: Represents European coalition discipline, sanctions, and defensive support.

players.label: Israel
players.victory_condition: Degrade Iranian nuclear, missile, and proxy capabilities while preventing a successful retaliatory campaign or northern-front collapse.
players.notes: Can act decisively but creates backlash and broader escalation risk.

players.label: Iran
players.victory_condition: Survive intact, preserve regime control, restore deterrence, maintain proxy leverage, and force a ceasefire or sanctions relief.
players.notes: Can trade conventional weakness for asymmetric coercion and domestic repression.

players.label: China
players.victory_condition: Protect energy access, expand diplomatic leverage, constrain US freedom of action, and preserve channels to Tehran.
players.notes: Seeks to shape ceasefire language, energy flows, and sanctions outcomes rather than direct combat.

players.label: Russia
players.victory_condition: Exploit the crisis to weaken US/EU influence, preserve residual Iranian alignment, and gain diplomatic or energy-market advantage.
players.notes: Uses diplomacy, obstruction, and limited matériel support rather than direct battlefield intervention.

aor_boundaries.description: Middle East theater including the Gulf and Levant.
Israel, Lebanon, Syria, Jordan, and nearby cross-border operating areas.
Persian Gulf maritime chokepoint and regional energy routes.
Iranian sovereign territory and strategic depth.

bases.notes: Primary regional air and maritime hub.
National command and air defense center.
Regime command node and repression hub.
Proxy logistics and political access point.
External diplomatic support and economic signaling.
External support, information, and veto leverage.

proxy_forces.label: Hezbollah
proxy_forces.scope: Northern deterrence and cross-border rocket force

proxy_forces.label: Iraqi militia network
proxy_forces.scope: Diversionary strikes and US base harassment

victory_conditions.value: Blue wins if Iran's nuclear risk is reduced to low, proxy network is degraded, coalition cohesion remains intact, and the Strait of Hormuz is reopened by the end of turn 5.
Red wins if Iran survives with regime cohesion, keeps at least partial proxy and maritime leverage, and forces a ceasefire or sanctions relief without losing core command authority.
PRC wins if it preserves energy access, avoids severe supply disruption, and gains measurable diplomatic leverage over ceasefire terms or sanctions relief.
RU wins if it expands diplomatic obstruction or energy-market advantage while preventing a unified Western settlement against its interests.

private_conditions.description: Only playable after a major Iranian missile or drone strike.
private_conditions.expression: requires Iranian direct strike this turn or previous turn

value.title: Integrated Air Defense Coalition
value.description: Deploy US sensors, interceptors, and command links to reinforce Israeli and regional air defense against Iranian salvos.

unopposed_rule.description: If unopposed, improve coalition defense but consume interceptors and raise Iranian retaliation intent.

response.cost_rule: Red may respond with saturation strike or deception.

value.notes: Defensive support with a political cost; helps credibility but not for free.

private_conditions.description: Requires heightened nuclear risk or evidence of breakout.

value.title: B-2 Penetrating Strike
value.description: Conduct a US long-range strike on buried Iranian nuclear facilities.

unopposed_rule.description: If unopposed, devastate nuclear infrastructure but sharply increase regional blowback and legal pressure.

response.cost_rule: Red may trigger retaliation or diplomatic condemnation.

value.title: Naval Quarantine of Iranian Ports
value.description: Apply maritime pressure to reduce Iran's ability to use sea lanes for revenue or coercion.

unopposed_rule.description: If unopposed, improve maritime stability for Blue but raise global energy shock.

response.cost_rule: Red may challenge with blockade, sanctions evasion, or diplomatic pushback.

value.notes: Secures sea lanes but can worsen prices.

value.title: War Powers Backlash
value.description: Congressional and media scrutiny of open-ended war measures raises political costs for sustained escalation.

unopposed_rule.description: If unopposed, reduce US freedom of action next turn.

international_event.title: Two-Week Temporary Ceasefire Proposal
international_event.description: Broker a fragile ceasefire imposing stand-downs, humanitarian access, and maritime pause measures.

unopposed_rule.description: If unopposed, reduce escalation and stabilize trade briefly.

response.cost_rule: Opponents may demand terms or attach conditions.

marker_chits.label: Escalation
marker_chits.description: Tracks intensity of direct and indirect conflict.

marker_chits.label: Coalition Cohesion
marker_chits.description: Tracks political unity among Blue partners.

marker_chits.label: Nuclear Risk
marker_chits.description: Tracks perceived breakout and weaponization danger.

marker_chits.label: Proxy Network
marker_chits.description: Tracks Iranian proxy cohesion and reach.

marker_chits.label: Maritime/Energy Stability
marker_chits.description: Tracks chokepoint access and oil/gas continuity.

marker_chits.label: Humanitarian/Legal Pressure
marker_chits.description: Tracks civilian harm, legitimacy, and lawfare.
